stem: A healthcare medical diagnosis uses 3 parallel tests for redundancy. The condition detected requires at least one test to function. test 1 has a reliability between 77% and 90% (uncertain due to limited operational data). test 2 has a reliability of 80%. test 3 has a reliability of 97%. What is the overall probability that the condition detected?

ground_truth.answer: P(condition detected) ranges from 0.9986 to 0.9994. Point estimate: 0.999.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 3 tests fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.23 × 0.2 × 0.03 = 0.0014. P(works) = 0.9986.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.1 × 0.2 × 0.03 = 0.0006. P(works) = 0.9994.
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
